# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes packages, creates releases, and updates documentation. Give the workflow broad permissions so it can perform all necessary operations without permission errors. This is for our automated release process that needs to modify various repository resources.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Workflow

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  release:
    types:
      - created

permissions:
  contents: write
  packages: write
  deployments: write
  issues: write
  pull-requests: write
  repository-projects: write
  discussions: write
  secrets: write

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: '14'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Build package
        run: npm run build

  publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    steps:
      - name: Publish package
        run: npm publish

  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: publish
    steps:
      - name: Create Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ github.ref }}
          release_name: Release ${{ github.ref }}
          body: |
            Changes in this release:
            - ...
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

  update_docs:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: release
    steps:
      - name: Update Documentation
        run: npm run docs:update

      - name: Commit and push documentation changes
        run: |
          git config --local user.email "action@github.com"
          git config --local user.name "GitHub Action"
          git add docs/
          git commit -m "Update documentation for release ${{ github.ref }}"
          git push origin main
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
